content:
{
  "title": "Meta-Prompting: Adaptive Prompt Selection for Mathematical Reasoning in LLMs",
  "problem": "Large language models often fail to select the optimal reasoning strategy when solving diverse mathematical problems, leading to inconsistent accuracy.",
  "motivation": "Current prompting methods—like chain-of-thought—work well in aggregate, but individual problems often require different prompting strategies. Improving per-instance prompt selection could help LLMs generalize better to unseen math problems, increasing their reliability for educational and scientific applications.",
  "method": "We propose a meta-prompting approach that first presents the LLM with a short list of available prompting strategies (e.g., chain-of-thought, scratchpad, direct answer, self-verification) and a given math problem. The LLM is first asked to select, with justification, which strategy it predicts will yield the correct answer. It is then presented with the problem using the chosen strategy. This explicit self-selection mechanism leverages the LLM’s internal knowledge of its own strengths and limitations to adaptively guide its reasoning approach.",
  "experiment_plan": "Test on GSM8K and MATH datasets, comparing standard prompting, chain-of-thought, and our meta-prompting method. The primary metric will be solution accuracy; secondary metrics include reasoning trace quality and self-selection justification plausibility. Baseline will be static chain-of-thought prompting. A small-scale pilot (100 problems) will validate whether meta-prompting yields higher accuracy than the best static baseline."
}
